headline: Monetizr Appoints Veteran Growth Strategist Michelle Suttle as Chief Growth Officer to Accelerate Growth in a New Era of Media
summary: ATLANTA, April 27, 2026--Monetizr today announced the appointment of Michelle Suttle as Chief Growth Officer, bringing on a seasoned growth leader as brands look for more effective ways to reach consumers beyond traditional digital channels.

content.title: Monetizr Appoints Veteran Growth Strategist Michelle Suttle as Chief Growth Officer to Accelerate Growth in a New Era of Media
content.summary: ATLANTA, April 27, 2026--Monetizr today announced the appointment of Michelle Suttle as Chief Growth Officer, bringing on a seasoned growth leader as brands look for more effective ways to reach consumers beyond traditional digital channels.

thumbnail.caption: Monetizr today announced the appointment of Michelle Suttle as Chief Growth Officer, bringing on a seasoned growth leader as brands look for more effective ways to reach consumers beyond traditional digital channels. Suttle joins Monetizr from Tangible, where she served as Chief Growth Officer, and brings more than two decades of experience across WPP and VML. Over the course of her career, she has led growth and marketing strategy for global brands including Barilla, Coca-Cola, Ferrari, GlaxoSmithKline, Haleon, and Keurig.

headline: Coca-Cola Names New People Chief As Talent Shapes Growth Story
summary: Coca-Cola (NYSE:KO) has appointed Tapaswee Chandele as Global Chief People Officer, effective May 1, 2026. Chandele has held senior human resources roles across multiple regions within the company. Outgoing CPO Lisa Chang will remain as a senior advisor and continue to serve on the board of The Coca-Cola Foundation. Coca-Cola, the global beverage company behind its flagship soft drink and a broad portfolio of brands, is making this leadership change at a time when talent, culture, and...

headline: Can Coca-Cola Retain Pricing Power and Regain Momentum
summary: Investors are watching Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO) ahead of Q1 2026 results due before the market opens Tuesday, April 28. After Keurig Dr Pepper (NASDAQ:KDP) posted stronger-than-expected results pointing to durable beverage pricing and resilient demand, this print will test whether KO’s momentum is still intact. headline: Tuesday earnings to watch: Coca-Cola, GM, Robinhood, Starbucks
summary: Yahoo Finance's Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Tuesday, Apr. 28. A huge slate of earnings is on deck, including Coca-Cola (KO), Spotify (SPOT), General Motors (GM), Starbucks (SBUX), and Robinhood (HOOD).The latest consumer confidence reading will be published in the morning.

content.title: Tuesday earnings to watch: Coca-Cola, GM, Robinhood, Starbucks
content.description:
Yahoo Finance's Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Tuesday, Apr. 28.

A huge slate of earnings is on deck, including Coca-Cola (KO), Spotify (SPOT), General Motors (GM), Starbucks (SBUX), and Robinhood (HOOD).

The latest consumer confidence reading will be published in the morning.
